text.title: help-documentation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for help-documentation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agents generate plausible but inaccurate step-by-step instructions when they lack ground-truth product screenshots or current UI state
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without access to the live product, agents cannot verify that steps match actual UI elements or account for real state transitions.

failure-mode.description: Generated documentation goes stale immediately after product changes — no self-updating mechanism without a pipeline
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Static documentation quickly diverges from reality; version-pinning screenshots and maintaining a content update schedule are manual processes.

failure-mode.description: Contextual help placement requires UI/UX judgment that agents cannot make from text descriptions alone
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents cannot visualize the layout; human designers must validate that help content appears in the right location without creating visual clutter.

failure-mode.description: Agents cannot verify that help content is accessible (screen reader, keyboard navigation) without running automated tools
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Accessibility must be tested with actual assistive technology; text descriptions alone cannot verify readability or usability for users with disabilities.

failure-mode.description: Help audit tables generated from requirement lists miss implicit user confusion patterns — support tickets are a better input
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Real users encounter edge cases and confusing state transitions that requirements documents do not capture.

failure-mode.description: Agents cannot verify that generated step-by-step instructions match the actual current UI
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always require a human QA pass against the live product before publishing.

failure-mode.description: Generated FAQ answers are often correct in aggregate but wrong in specific edge cases
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Human review required before publishing; test with actual users if possible.

failure-mode.description: Agents producing onboarding tour copy do not know which UI elements exist or their current labels
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Provide a component inventory as input; require verification against the running product.

failure-mode.description: Direct publication of agent-generated documentation without editorial review
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Treat all agent output as first draft requiring editorial review before publishing.

failure-mode.description: Agents will invent plausible troubleshooting steps that do not correspond to real error states
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require a list of actual error codes/states as input to the agent prompt.
